title: Dirty crude oil flows to China
section_path: Dirty crude oil flows to China
section_path_text: Dirty crude oil flows to China
summary:
Main
Snapshot of Crude and Product Freight Rates, Supply-Demand
Week 24, June 14, 2024
Image reference: Στιγμιότυπο οθόνης 2024-06-14 134600.png | assets/2024-06-14_dirty-crude-oil-flows-to-china_img_cea3cf84ceb9ceb3cebcceb9cf8ccf84cf85_0013fd0b8a3d.png
In the first half of May, there was a weakening trend in dirty freight rates for the VLCC AG-China market, along with a significant downward revision on the Aframax Med route. On the demand side, the growth in dirty and clean tonne days has yet to show an increasing pace. Consequently, the supply of vessels will play a critical role in maintaining the robust environment for dirty and clean freight rates in the coming days.

Embedded info:
dpi: (119.9896, 119.9896)

OCR text:
Chart of the Week: Dirty crude oil flows to China
This week's chart highlights the declining trend in the quarterly volume of dirty oil flows to
China for the second quarter of this year, primarily driven by a slowdown in Russian oil
exports compared to last year's record pace. In contrast, Saudi Arabia has maintained a
strong pace, with quarterly volumes not falling below last year's levels.
